items.title: Indian envoy to China visits Tibet to review arrangements for Kailash Manasarovar pilgrims
items.summary: The yatra resumed in June 2025 after the first batch of Indian pilgrims arrived at Manasarovar Lake in Tibet after a five-year hiatus

items.title: British High Commissioner Lindy Cameron pays tribute to Air India plane crash victims
items.summary: ‘One year ago today, the tragic Air India crash claimed 260 lives, including 52 British nationals,’ British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron said in a post on X

items.title: Tehran would not cede control of Strait of Hormuz under draft U.S. deal, says Iran
items.summary: The Mehr news agency published a draft deal with the United States which would, if adopted, secure the release of $24 billion in frozen assets within a 60-day period

items.summary: U.S. forces shoot down two Iranian attack drones, an official says; Iran said it had not reached a final decision on a deal with Washington after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a "great settlement" to end the war

items.title: Thailand princess Bajrakitiyabha dies at 47
items.summary: Bajrakitiyabha Mahidol died on the evening of June 11 at a Bangkok hospital where she had been cared for since falling unconscious due to illness three years ago

items.title: Dinesh Trivedi reaches Bangladesh ahead of taking charge as High Commissioner
items.summary: High Commissioner-designate calls for stronger India-Bangladesh cooperation in health, technology and education as he arrives in Dhaka

items.title: Johannesburg shooting: At least 12 killed, 9 injured in late-night mass shooting

items.summary: The ‌shootings took place in three separate areas in central Israel close to the Palestinian West Bank city of Qalqilya.

items.title: Nine killed, 10 injured as 12 unknown suspects open fire in South Africa: police
items.summary: The attackers in two vehicles “opened fire at tavern patrons and continued to shoot randomly as they fled the scene,” a police statement said

items.title: False AI 'fact-checks' stir online chaos after Charlie Kirk assassination
items.summary: A day after Charlie Kirk, a 31-year-old prominent ally of U.S. President Donald Trump, was fatally gunned down at a university in Utah, the X account of AI chatbot Perplexity falsely stated that the activist was never shot

items.title: Bondi gunmen conducted ‘tactical’ training in countryside, police say
items.summary: Police have alleged that two men accused of carrying out last week’s deadly mass shooting on Bondi beach trained for the attack in the Australian countryside

items.title: Tumbler Ridge shooting: Timeline of the incident
items.summary: At least nine people were killed and 26 got injured in a mass shooting in a Canada school. The suspected attacker, a female, ended her life in self-inflicted gun shot, said police

items.title: Suspect dead after approaching White House checkpoint with weapon, says U.S. Secret Service
items.summary: The U.S. Secret Service said in a statement that, according to a preliminary investigation, the person approached a checkpoint shortly after 6 p.m. ET, “removed a weapon from his bag and began firing at posted officers”.

items.title: Trump backs off from more strikes after U.S., Iran exchange fire
items.summary: Earlier in the day, Mr. Trump had written in another post that he would continue striking Iran after both sides exchanged fire for two consecutive nights. After Thursday’s attack, Iran also announced that it closed the Strait of Hormuz to all traffc.

items.title: Strike cuts power to Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant: IAEA
items.summary: No release of radioactivity was detected and radiation levels remained normal, the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said in a statement

items.title: Opening delayed for U.S.-Canada bridge threatened by Trump

items.title: Solomon Islands says China security pact to remain secret
items.summary: Speaking last week in Australia, the Solomons' new Prime Minister Matthew Wale said his Cabinet would review the deal, which he admitted he had not seen until just before his visit

items.title: Pentagon lockdown over ‘hazardous materials’ incident due to false alarm: report
items.summary: “The systems have ​detected an air quality issue necessitating precautionary measures ⁠until we determine its significance,” Pentagon spokesperson Sean Parnell said in an ‌email

items.title: UN mission in Afghanistan confirms death toll of 13 civilians in Pakistani airstrikes
22 countries tell Iran to stop attacks 'on our soil'
items.summary: "Attempts to kill, kidnap, harass, intimidate, or otherwise attack people on our soil, undermines national sovereignty and international norms. These actions must stop immediately," the countries said in a joint statement

items.title: U.K. Defence Secretary John Healey quits over low funding

items.summary: From the deaths of three Indian seafarers in a U.S. strike near Oman to Kerala’s Nipah virus alert and the Cockroach Janta Party’s education manifesto - Top stories of the day.

items.summary: The current forecast reduced the expected ‌oil demand growth this year from 1.17 million barrels ​per day seen previously

items.summary: The talks come as Seoul is pursuing a transfer of wartime operational control from Washington, a process that has raised questions over ​how the allies would coordinate nuclear planning and conventional force

items.summary: Mahender Makhijani has been living in the U.S. on a Green Card and faces a maximum sentence of 30 years in prison if convicted

items.summary: Union Shipping Minister Sarbananda Sonowal said he has directed officials to ensure immediate repatriation of the rescued crew members and swift return of the mortal remains of the deceased for their final rites

items.title: Bangkok shrine bombing case 2015: Thailand sentences Chinese Uyghurs to death
items.summary: A Thai court convicted Yusufu Mieraili and Bilal Mohammed of premeditated and attempted murder for their role in planting a bomb at Erawan Shrine in Bangkok

items.summary: Two 'violating ships' attempting passage through Hormuz hit, Iranian media says; Iran's UN ambassador Amir Saeid Iravani rejected Trump's threat, saying "no sustainable deal can be reached through threats, intimidation, or the use of force."

items.summary: India condemns attack, summons U.S. Embassy’s Deputy Chief of Mission Jason Meeks to lodge a “strong protest”

items.title: Ex-intelligence chief Sallay ‘directed’ 2019 Easter bombings, says Sri Lanka Minister
items.summary: Minister Ananda Wijepala said Major General Suresh Sallay, who was arrested in February on accusations of “aiding and abetting” the attack, had identified a Catholic church to be bombed

items.title: UNHCR says fewer people displaced worldwide in 2025 but long-term refugee crisis persists
items.summary: Most returns ‌were to six countries: the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, Syria, Afghanistan, ‌Ukraine ⁠and Myanmar

items.title: FBI seizes 13 websites allegedly used by China to target, recruit U.S. workers
items.summary: The 13 websites purported to be affiliated with consulting companies that advertised job openings for current and former holders of security clearances. But the companies were all fakes and the job postings were a sham, officials said

items.title: Iran says ‘will target’ any vessel traffic in Strait of Hormuz after U.S. strikes in latest escalation
items.summary: U.S. Central Command said in a social media post that the military is striking “multiple targets in Iran” and that it's being done “in response to Iran's unwarranted and continued aggression”.

items.title: Erdogan says Israel's attacks on Syria, Lebanon threaten Turkey too
items.summary: NATO member Turkey has been one of the fiercest critics of Israel’s assaults on Iran, Gaza, ‌and Lebanon, saying Israel was the biggest obstacle to regional peace. It has halted ‌all trade with Israel and called for measures ‌against ⁠it at international courts

items.title: Ukraine launches long-range strikes on military, energy sites in Russia
items.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country's forces struck several military and energy infrastructure sites, including a military factory that he said supplied components for Russian drones and missiles

items.title: Pakistan says carried out air strikes at terror hideouts in Afghanistan, 26 killed
items.summary: “Precise and calibrated strikes were carried out along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border areas on hideouts and safe havens of masterminds and planners belonging to Fitna Al Khwarij, killing 26 khawarijs,” says Information Minister

items.title: Israeli strikes on south Lebanon kill 12: medical source
items.summary: "The number of martyrs from the Israeli airstrikes in the town of Tayr Dibba is eight, and in Deir Qanun al-Nahr it is four," the source said, speaking on condition of anonymity
